name: Potato
description: Potato (Solanum tuberosum) is a major starchy tuber crop and a key food-security crop in Bangladesh and South Asia.

diseases.name: Late Blight
diseases.description: A destructive blight causing fast foliar and tuber rot under cool, humid conditions.
symptoms: Water-soaked dark green spots on leaf tips and margins
White cottony growth on leaf undersides in humid weather
Reddish-brown dry rot develops inside tubers

diseases.name: Early Blight
diseases.description: A common leaf blight with concentric ring lesions that starts on older foliage.
symptoms: Small dark brown target-like spots on older lower leaves
Disease progresses upward in warm, dry conditions

diseases.name: Black Scurf
diseases.description: A soil-borne disease causing stem lesions, poor emergence, and black sclerotia on tubers.
symptoms: Reddish-brown lesions on sprouts and stolons
Stunted plants and poor stand establishment
treatments: Treat seed with Carbendazim (0.1%) or Boric Acid (3%)
Use 3-year crop rotation with non-solanaceous crops

diseases.name: Fusarium Wilt / Dry Rot
diseases.description: A wilt and tuber dry rot complex causing vascular discoloration and post-harvest losses.
symptoms: Sunken wrinkled tubers with dry internal rot

diseases.name: Powdery Scab
diseases.description: A tuber and root disease causing warty lesions that release powdery spore masses.
symptoms: Warty galls on tuber skin that rupture
Powdery brown spore mass in lesions
treatments: Rely on prevention because chemical control is limited

diseases.name: Silver Scurf
diseases.description: A storage-associated tuber skin disease causing silver discoloration and moisture loss.
symptoms: Silvery-brown tuber skin discoloration from stolon end
Tubers shrink due to moisture loss

diseases.name: Charcoal Rot
diseases.description: A heat and drought-associated stem and tuber rot caused by sclerotial fungus.
symptoms: Premature vine death during hot dry weather
Fine black sclerotia in vascular tissue

diseases.name: Verticillium Wilt (Early Dying)
diseases.description: A vascular wilt that causes premature senescence and yield loss.
symptoms: Premature yellowing and death of lower leaves

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.description: A severe vascular bacterial wilt causing sudden collapse of plants.
symptoms: Sudden plant wilting without early yellowing
Milky bacterial ooze from cut stems in water
treatments: Use certified true potato seed or clean seed tubers

diseases.name: Common Scab
diseases.description: A tuber skin disease causing corky scab lesions and reduced market value.
symptoms: Rough corky scabs or pits on tuber surface
Blemished appearance with mostly unaffected interior
treatments: Avoid heavy liming before and during potato season

diseases.name: Blackleg / Soft Rot
diseases.description: A bacterial stem base and tuber soft rot complex, especially severe in wet conditions.
symptoms: Black decay from seed piece upward
treatments: Store tubers in cool dry ventilated conditions

diseases.name: Potato Leaf Roll (PLRV)
diseases.pathogen: Potato leafroll virus; vector: Myzus persicae
diseases.description: A virus disease causing upward leaf rolling, brittleness, and yield loss.
symptoms: Leaves roll upward and become stiff
treatments: Plant early to avoid peak aphid pressure

diseases.name: Potato Virus Y (PVY)
diseases.description: A major potato virus causing mosaic, distortion, and severe yield impact.

diseases.name: Potato Virus X (PVX)
diseases.pathogen: Potato virus X; spread: contact and mechanical
diseases.description: A mechanically transmitted virus that is mild alone but severe in mixed infections.
symptoms: Severe symptoms when co-infected with PVY
treatments: Control aphids to limit mixed infections

diseases.name: Potato Virus S (PVS)
diseases.description: A usually mild or latent virus that can still reduce yield.
treatments: Maintain isolation between seed and ware crops

diseases.name: Root Knot
diseases.description: A nematode disease causing root and tuber galling, stunting, and quality loss.
treatments: Apply approved nematicide according to local regulations
Use neem cake as soil amendment
Rotate with marigold and non-host crops

diseases.name: Potato Cyst Nematode (PCN)
diseases.description: A quarantine-significant nematode pest causing patchy decline and major tuber yield loss.
symptoms: Tiny white to brown cysts on roots
treatments: Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 6+ years
